semanticCore: Ritual, sacrifice, piety, devotion, lifestyle, or silver
quranicRelevance: Rites/rituals of Hajj/worship (Manasik - 'wa arina manasikana'), and sacrificial offering (Nusuk - 'fa-fidyatun min siyamin... aw nusukin', 'inna salatee wa nusukee').

keyDerivations.meaning: Sacrifice, sacrificial animal, devotion, piety, or offering
Place/rite of sacrifice, sanctuary, Hajj ritual, or devotional path

figurativeOrRareUsages: قُلْ إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ (6:162): Say, 'Indeed, my prayer, my sacrifice/devotion, my living and my dying are for God'.
discardedNoise: Ascetic monks (Nasik) in post-canonical Christian studies.
summaryEn: The root represents religious ritual and sacrifice (Nusuk), spanning Hajj rites (Manasik), animal sacrifice compensations, and holistic devotional lifestyles.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر العبادة وإراقة الدم تقرباً، واصفاً مناسك الحج الباقية (مناسكنا)، والفدية بالذبح (نسك)، وتفويض الحياة نسكاً لله.
rawText: أَنْسَكَ 4 [app. He washed and purified a garment]. (TA voce إِجْتَابَ.) تَنَسَّكَ 5 He devoted himself to religious exercises; applied himself to devotion; (S, Msb, K:) as also 🡻نَسَكَ (S, K) and 🡻نَسُكَ: (K:) or the last, he became a نَاسِك. (S.) مَنَاسِكُ الحَجِّ The religious rites and ceremonies of the pilgrimage: or the places where those rites and ceremonies are performed. (Msb.) نَسَكَ 1 He worshipped: used transitively. See an ex. in a verse of El-Aashà, in the S, art. نصب. See 5. نَسِيكَةٌ : see عَقِيقَةٌ.
